heading: Property records: maintenance on quantitative and monetary basis
label: 2721 Property records: maintenance on quantitative and monetary basis
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)2701(a)2701(b)5:172i (less last sentence).5:172i (last sentence).; added , .July 26, 1947, ch. 343, § 410Aug. 10, 1949, ch. 412, § 11(410)63 Stat. 590In subsection (a), the words “equipment” and “materials” are omitted, since the word “supplies”, as defined in , includes equipment and materials. The word “stored” is substituted for the words “held in store by the armed services”.section 101(26) of this titleIn subsection (b), the words “on property records maintained under this section” are substituted for the word “thereon”.

Amendments1991—, substituted section catchline for one which read “Basis: reports”.Pub. L. 102–190, § 1061(a)(17)(A), designated existing provisions as subsec. (a) and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 102–190, § 347(b)1990— struck out “(a)” before “Under regulations” and struck out subsec. (b) which read as follows: “The Secretary shall report once a year to Congress and the President on property records maintained under this section.”Pub. L. 101–510

Implementation of 1991 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 102–190, div. A, title III, § 347(c)105 Stat. 1347Dec. 5, 1991“The Secretary of Defense shall establish the uniform system of valuation described in , United States Code (as added by subsection (a)), and prescribe the regulations required by section 2721(b) of such title (as added by subsection (b)), not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”section 2458(a)(3) of title 10Dec. 5, 1991

Inventory Investigations, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–456, div. A, title III, § 343102 Stat. 1961Sept. 29, 1988“(a).—Undercover Investigations(1)Congress finds that the use of undercover investigative techniques by the Department of Defense enhances the ability of the Department of Defense to detect and investigate theft of Government property (including munitions) from the Department of Defense supply system.“(2)The Secretary of Defense is urged to continue to conduct undercover investigations to detect and investigate thefts referred to in paragraph (1).“(b).—Inventory Security Incident RepositoryThe Secretary of Defense shall establish and maintain a centralized computer system for recording and organizing information on theft, fraud, and breach of security and incidents involving the loss of Department of Defense supplies (including munitions).”

children.label: a
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by him, the Secretary of Defense shall have the records of the fixed property, installations, major equipment items, and stored supplies of the military departments maintained on both a quantitative and a monetary basis, so far as practicable.
to the extent practicable, provide up-to-date information on all items in the inventory of the Department of Defense;
indicate whether the inventory of each item is sufficient or excessive in relation to the needs of the Department for that item; and

children.label: A
children.text: the amounts proposed for each appropriation account in such budget for inventory purchases of the Department of Defense; and

children.label: B
children.text: the amounts obligated for such inventory purchases out of the corresponding appropriations account for the preceding fiscal year.
